folio.label: 179r
folio.l: Thurgú whicú he slow hi~ self , for wrecchednesse
¢ Sampson , this noble , almygúty champiou~
Witú outen wepene , saue hise handes tweye
He slow , and al to rent the leou~
Toward his weddyngö , walkyngö by the weye
His fals wyf , coude hi~ so plese & preye
Til she his counseille knew , and she vntrewe
Vn to hise foos , his counseille gan bywreye
And hi~ forsoke , and toke a nother newe
¢ Thre hondred foxes , toke Sampson for Ire
And alle here tayles , he to gidre bond
And sette the foxes tailles , alle on fire
For he on euäy taille , had knyt a brond
And they brente alle the cornes , in æ lond
And alle here Olyuerys , & vynes eke
A thousand men , he slow eke w his hond
And had no wepene , but an asses cheke
¢ Whan they were slayn , so thursted hi~ , æ he
Was wel ney lorn , for whicú he gan to preye
That god wolde on his peyne , han som pitee
And sende hi~ drynk , or elles most he deye
And of this Asses cheke , that was dreye
Out of a wangö totú , sprangö a noon a welle
Of whicú he drank I now , shortly to seye
Thus halp hi~ god , as Iudicu~ can telle
¢ By verrey force , at Gazan on a nygút
Maugree the Philistiens , of that Citee
The gates of the Toun~ , he hatú vp pligút
And oon his bak , I caried hem hatú he
Heigú on an hil , where as men mygút he~ se
O noble almygúty , Sampsou~ lief and dere
Had æè nat tolde , to wo~men thy secree
In al this world , ne hadde ben thy pere
¢ This Sampsou~ , neuäe , sither drank ne wyn
Ne on his hed cam rasour noon , ne shere
By päcept , of the Messager dyuyn
For alle hise strengthes , in hise heres were
And fully twenty wynter , ©ere by ©ere
He hadde of Israel , the gouänaunce
But sone shal he wepe , many a tere
For wo~men shuln hi~ brynge , to myschaunce
¢ Vn to his lemman Dalida , he tolde
That in hise heeris , al his strengthe lay
